channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: At least 32 dead in massive US storm after new fatalities reported in Kansas and Mississippi
item.description: PIEDMONT, Mo. (AP) — Violent tornadoes ripped through parts of the U.S., wiping out schools and toppling semitractor-trailers in several states, part of a monster storm that has killed at least 32 people as more severe weather was expected late Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JEFF ROBERSON, REBECCA REYNOLDS and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump invokes Alien Enemies Act in mass deportation effort
item.description: The president’s move comes hours after a judge temporarily blocked the removal of 5 Venezuelans who the Trump administration says are members of Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Doechii joins Will Smith to recreate viral TikTok trend
item.description: The trend uses her song "Anxiety" and spoofs a scene from "The Fresh Prince of Bell-Air" where Smith's character sneaks up on Ali's, who's dancing with her headphones on, and starts to mimic her.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Nearly six in 10 people, 59 percent, said it was “not too" or “not at all likely” that Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine war will bring long-term peace between the two Eastern European countries. Around 41 percent disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Oklahoma man says Social Security benefits terminated without warning or explanation
item.description: A local retiree says his Social Security benefits were suddenly suspended without warning — and with no explanation given when he reached out. He worries it may have to do with the place he was born, and ongoing DOGE cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, KRON4

item.title: Trump pledges to 'expose' his enemies in political speech at Justice Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump pledged to “expose” his enemies during a norm-breaking political speech Friday at the Justice Department in which he aired a litany of grievances about the criminal cases he faced and vowed retribution for what he described as the “lies and abuses that have o
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More than 50 universities face federal investigations as part of Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 50 universities are being investigated for alleged racial discrimination as part of President Donald Trump’s campaign to end diversity, equity and inclusion programs that his officials say exclude white and Asian American students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man escorted out of NC Republican's town hall
item.description: Rep. Chuck Edwards (R-N.C.) faced a wave of backlash from constituents while hosting a Thursday town hall in Asheville, which he held against the advice of party leaders. One attendee, who identified himself as a veteran, was removed from the event. “He has nothing to say but lies.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Viral 'American Idol' contestant dead at 32, family says
item.description: Douglas Kiker, the garbage collector who touched hearts with his singing voice on "American Idol" has died, his family announced on social media. He was 32 years old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge considers blocking DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge is considering whether to temporarily block Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Social Security Administration systems that hold sensitive data on millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cause of death for Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa's dog released
item.description: An examination of the dog found dead along with actor Gene Hackman and his wife in their Santa Fe home shows dehydration and starvation were likely what led to the animal’s demise.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Helicopters permanently restricted on route near Washington airport where 67 died in midair crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Helicopters will be permanently restricted from flying near Washington, D.C.'s airport on the same route where a passenger jet and an Army helicopter collided in midair, killing 67 people, the Federal Aviation Administration said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Big March storm system threatens US with tornadoes, blizzards and wildfire risk
item.description: A huge storm system crossing the U.S. threatens to unleash tornadoes Friday in the Mississippi Valley, blizzards in the northern Plains and dry, gusty conditions in Texas and Oklahoma that pose a high risk of wildfires.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Judge formally sentences Adnan Syed to time served in 'Serial' podcast case
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — A Maryland judge formally sentenced Adnan Syed on Friday to the time he's already served in prison, appearing to finally bring to a close a long-running case with numerous legal twists and turns that received worldwide attention from the true-crime podcast “Serial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DHS revokes visa of one Columbia student, arrests another pro-Palestinian protester
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) announced Friday it has revoked the visa of one Columbia University student and arrested another person involved in the pro-Palestinian protests, escalating its crackdown on those engaged in demonstrations over the conflict in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Where you live can change your chances of getting dementia: Study
item.description: The study shows that air pollution and access to green spaces can affect your odds of developing dementia.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Campbell, NewsNation

item.title: DHS agents search Columbia dorm rooms after Khalil arrest
item.description: Department of Homeland Security agents searched two dorm rooms at Columbia University Thursday night, the school said, a move that comes less than a week after the federal arrest of former grad student Mahmoud Khalil.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's tariff wars forge rare bipartisan alliance in Kentucky as bourbon makers fear escalation
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — President Donald Trump's blunt-force use of tariffs has forged a rare bipartisan alliance among Kentucky's top leaders — Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear and Republican Sens. Mitch McConnell and Rand Paul — in lamenting brewing trade wars that could stagger the bourbon sector.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tesla says it is 'exposed' to retaliatory tariffs
item.description: Tesla warned U.S. trade representative (USTR) Jamieson Greer that the electric vehicle company and other American exporters are “inherently exposed” to retaliatory tariffs levied in response to ones from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Education Department investigating dozens of universities after DEI warning letter
item.description: The Education Department announced Friday it is investigating more than 50 colleges and universities over what it called "racial preferences" in academics or scholarships, a move that comes amid the Trump administration's wider crackdown on diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 60 percent of voters unhappy with DOGE handling of federal workers: Poll
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE have been trying to slash their way through the federal government, offering buyouts to and laying off scores of workers. Many of their actions are now the subject of lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Newborn kittens rescued from wrecked car moments before demolition
item.description: A litter of kittens was rescued from the inside of a wrecked car at a junkyard moments before it was scheduled to be demolished Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Burn pit fund for veterans on chopping block in GOP spending bill
item.description: The fund helps former service members sickened by military toxic exposures, including burn pit smoke and Agent Orange contamination.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Johns Hopkins University cutting more than 2,000 workers due to federal aid cuts
item.description: “The termination of more than $800 million in [U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID)] funding is now forcing us to wind down critical work here in Baltimore and internationally,” a spokesperson for the school said in an email.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Former US Sen. Alan Simpson dies at age 93
item.description: Former U.S. Sen. Alan Simpson, a political legend whose quick wit bridged partisan gaps in the years before today’s political acrimony, has died. He was 93.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Johns Hopkins University cutting more than 2,000 workers due to federal aid cuts
item.description: Johns Hopkins University said Thursday that it is cutting over 2,000 workers because of a federal funding cut.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Third MLB hat pulled over accidental vulgarity as others sell for hundreds online
item.description: Yet another newly released MLB hat has been pulled from online stores after fans noted the potentially crass design the cap sported.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Second judge blocks mass firings of probationary federal workers
item.description: A second federal judge has blocked the Trump administration from firing federal workers, issuing an order Thursday that probationary employees be reinstated weeks after their dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: American Airlines Boeing 737 catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Columbine victim's 2025 death ruled a homicide after injuries from school shooting contributed to sepsis
item.description: The February 2025 death of Anne Marie Hochhalter, who was partially paralyzed when she was shot during the Columbine High School massacre, has been ruled a homicide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Justice Department is investigating whether Columbia University hid students sought by the US
item.description: A top U.S. Justice Department official says the department is investigating whether Columbia University concealed “illegal aliens” on its campus.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Once-routine immigration check-ins become high-stakes calculation as some are detained
item.description: When a couple from Colombia who was planning their wedding showed up for a check-in with U.S. immigration authorities, one was given his next appointment date. The other was detained and deported. Jhojan doesn’t know why Felipe was detained at the Feb.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, CEDAR ATTANASIO and SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US transfers immigrants out of Guantanamo Bay to Louisiana as court weighs legality
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. authorities have removed immigrants from detention facilities at the Guantanamo Bay naval base as a federal court in Washington weighs a challenge by civil rights advocates to holding immigrants at the offshore military station. A spokesperson for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Some universities are freezing hiring and laying off staff as Trump cuts federal funding
item.description: Universities across the U.S. have announced hiring freezes, citing new financial uncertainty as the Trump administration threatens a range of cuts to federal contracts and research grants. Some have announced layoffs.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 risks facing the US economy under Trump
item.description: Warning signs have been flashing and markets have been sliding amid policy uncertainties and some troubling macroeconomic indicators.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: SpaceX Dragon launch to relieve stranded astronauts is delayed again
item.description: Less than an hour before liftoff, the SpaceX Dragon mission to provide assistance to two astronauts was aborted yet again, this time for a ground equipment issue.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5
